steps.title: Determine what would weaken the claim
steps.content: The student claims joining an FTA increases a country's agricultural export growth rate. To weaken this, we need a case where growth was lower after joining an FTA than before — directly contradicting the claim.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — Over the five years after Costa Rica joined CAFTA-DR, agricultural exports from Costa Rica grew at a rate of about 13.5 percent, which is higher than the rate over the five years before Costa Rica joined the agreement.: Higher growth after joining supports the claim rather than weakening it. ✗
  • Choice B — All the countries shown had positive growth in agricultural exports over the five years after joining their respective FTAs, but their rates of export growth varied.: Confirms positive post-FTA growth with no before/after rate comparison; too neutral to weaken the claim. ✗
  • Choice C — Although agricultural exports from Jordan decreased over the five years before JOFTA, a reversal in this trend was observed over the five years after Jordan joined JOFTA.: A reversal to growth after joining supports the claim. ✗
  • Choice D — Although agricultural exports from Morocco grew over the five years after Morocco joined MAFTA, their growth rate was even higher in the five years before MAFTA.: Growth was lower after joining than before — directly contradicts the claim that FTAs raise growth rates. ✓
D is correct.
